sources.name: English Wikisource
sources.notes: Use MediaWiki API. Record oldid for stable citation.

sources.name: Chinese Wikisource
sources.notes: Use for Chinese classics when pages are complete and sourced.

sources.name: French Wikisource
sources.notes: Use for French originals and French public-domain translations.

sources.name: German Wikisource
sources.notes: Use for German originals and older German editions.

sources.name: Latin Wikisource
sources.notes: Use for Latin originals and early modern Latin texts.

sources.name: Greek Wikisource
sources.notes: Use as a secondary Greek source; prefer Perseus/Scaife for classical citation schemes when possible.

sources.name: Project Gutenberg
sources.notes: Gutendex is used as a lightweight Project Gutenberg discovery API. Verify item license text.

sources.name: Internet Archive
sources.notes: Check licenseurl and rights fields. Some items are borrow-only or restricted.

sources.name: Open Library
sources.notes: Use for work/edition metadata and Internet Archive links.

sources.name: HathiTrust
sources.notes: Use for edition discovery and Full View checks. Manual verification is required in this starter kit.

sources.name: Perseus / Scaife Viewer
sources.notes: Use for Greek and Latin originals with CTS URN citation. Manual lookup is included in this starter kit.

sources.name: Directory of Open Access Books
sources.notes: Use for peer-reviewed open-access books. Verify book-level license.

sources.name: OAPEN Library
sources.notes: Use for open-access monographs and chapters. Verify item license.

sources.name: Kanseki Repository
sources.notes: GitHub-backed Chinese text repository. Prefer raw files and record commit when possible.

sources.name: Chinese Text Project
sources.notes: Use as search and comparison aid. Avoid automated bulk extraction.

sources.name: Scribd
sources.notes: Do not extract or translate as final source without independent verification.
